title: Pterozonium elaphoglossoides
intro: Pterozonium elaphoglossoides is a type of fern, specifically a species of the Pterozonium genus, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterozonium elaphoglossoides include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pterozonium elaphoglossoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
